Title: NEW Operator
Description: This returns an instance (or reference to an instance) of the user-defined type `type_name`.
It will try to match a constructor by calculating the parameter types at compile time and matching the produced signature to the closest constructor signature.
SQL types have an implicit constructor with no parameters that assigns DEFAULT values to type members (if any), otherwise NULL.
When a SQL constructor is called, SELF is set to the result of calling the implicit constructor.
The NEW operator is a scalar expression and can be used wherever the SQL syntax allows scalar expressions.
